definition: The Fed's reaction function exhibits a dovish asymmetry where recession costs are politically salient but 2.8% inflation is not. Qualitative existence is supported by political economy reasoning, but the specific magnitude (~200bp) is estimated from a single observation (September 2024 cut at core PCE ~2.7%) with zero degrees of freedom.
evidence: Political economy argument: recession cost is politically salient, 2.8% inflation is not
September 2024 cut at core PCE ~2.7% as single observation
Multi-cycle evidence shows asymmetry is not structural constant — absent under Greenspan 1994-95, reversed under Volcker
Two-threshold estimation from two observations is a just-identified system
open_questions: Will the 2025-2026 framework review shift the asymmetry?
How do FOMC composition changes affect the reaction function?
Is this a permanent structural feature or regime/personnel-dependent?
